items.title: Home recording studio equipment for beginners
items.editorNotes: Rewritten for clarity and readability while maintaining factual accuracy and preserving all required links and images.
items.excerptBefore: If you are looking to build your own home studio in 2025, we have the perfect guide for you. Here you will find all the gear you need.
items.excerptAfter: A comprehensive guide for beginners looking to set up a home recording studio in 2025, covering essential equipment like microphones, audio interfaces, and MIDI keyboards.
items.seoTitleBefore: Home recording studio equipments for beginners
items.seoTitleAfter: Home Recording Studio Equipment for Beginners
items.seoDescriptionBefore: If you are looking to build your own home studio in 2025, we have the perfect guide for you. Here you will find all the gear you need.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Discover essential equipment for building your home recording studio in 2025. From microphones to audio interfaces, find everything you need to start…
items.beforeBody:
If you are looking to build your own home studio in 2025, we have the perfect guide for you. We've created a checklist for home recording studio equipment that you will need.

Here you will find all the gear you need to get started recording music right from the comfort of your bedroom.


Wires and cables

XLR cables

You may have a high-end condenser and good quality audio interface, but with a worn-out XLR cable, you risk having noise in your recording. Mechanical damage, corrosion, and dry out cracking can happen easily if you buy a cheap XLR cable.

We recommend Mogami Gold Studio — you can pick between 3, 6, 15, and 25-foot lengths.

  • comes in different lengths
  • recommended for studio recordings

TS vs TRS cables 

So what is the difference between the two, and which one do you need? TS cable connectors are perfect for instruments with mono signal — like guitars, mixers, or a receiver.

TRS cables have 3 contact points. They are commonly used to connect balanced equipment or for running both left and right mono signals to stereo headphones.


Microphones

Shure SM57 Dynamic microphone

The Shure SM57 is one of the most legendary recording microphones on the market. In fact, many award-winning albums have been recorded with just a single SM57, making this the absolute best choice for a versatile mic that can do everything.

The SM57 is best suited for instruments, but can also work well with vocals. It gives off a very neutral sound and does the job, keeping the voice clean and clear without and with no pop. If you want to take it up a notch on making it clean, consider a mic stand and a pop filter.

  • Perfect price range for the quality of sound
  • High loudness tolerance
  • Die-cast steel


RODE NT1-A

If you want an incredible dynamic range, this is the all-around mic for you. You can mic up your drums or even a quiet talking voice — you can be sure that RODE NT1-A will give you a clear, quiet, and clean sound. It has a 1-year warranty that you can extend to 10 years upon registration.

It comes with a RODE SM6 shock mount, pop filter, and dust cover.

  • Dynamic range
  • Gets rid of hiss from grounded mics
  • Comes with a full microphone setup


Audio interface

Audio interfaces allow you to connect and play with pro mics and instruments, making them an essential component of your home studio.

Scarlett USB interface

  • Offers great value for money
  • Several versions depending on the amount of input that you need
  • Easy to set up

Our easiest suggestion is the Focusrite Scarlett Studio. If you want to set up, record and play back within 5 minutes — this is the best one. It just simply does what it's supposed to do, without fail.

It also comes with a range of software: Avid Pro Tools, Ableton, Softube, and even a 3-month splice sounds subscription.

If you choose the Scarlett 2i2 Studio, the interface comes with a CM25 MKIII condenser microphone, and HP60 MkIII

DAW

Are you looking for the best value DAW subscriptions or even free ones? We’ve covered almost all of DAW software available! Check out our in-depth review for DAW tools here.

Read the best DAWs in 2025

Great budget monitor

If you are really looking into creating quality recording, mixing or mastering, having good monitors will is the perfect addition to your home recording studio equipment.

Adam Audio T5V

This is a highly affordable two-way monitor, perfect for smaller rooms or a home studio. It has top-end EQ adjustment and is very customizable. It also allows balanced and unbalanced connections.

Yamaha HS5

Another 5” woofer that we love! This size is perfect for a home recording studio. With the HS5, Yamaha had both professionals and beginners in mind, yet was still able to keep it at an affordable price. It has excellent build quality and a great frequency response that allows for excellent mixing and mastering. Clean and refined design also allows your studio space to have a professional feel.

MIDI Keyboard

Arturia MiniLab Mk II

Just with a glance, you can already tell that MinilabMKII has sturdy and high-quality keys, knobs, pads, pitch, and modulation. Some parts of the case are made out of metal, which gives it a good heft and a solid feel.

It also comes with hundreds of free virtual instruments that you can download. Ableton Lite host software is also included, but is limited to creating a maximum of eight tracks.

 

Read the best MIDI Keyboard

items.seoTitleBefore: 5 best music courses online 2025
items.seoTitleAfter: 5 Best Online Music Courses for 2025
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Music learning is more accessible than ever! Here are our top picks of music courses online, for music theory, production, and learning.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Discover the top 5 online music courses for 2025, including offerings from Musical U, Timbaland, Udemy, Berklee College of Music, and Soundfly. Enhance…
items.beforeBody:
Gone are the days when learning music was limited to The Juilliard School or Berklee College. Music is more accessible than ever before, with tons of online music courses, tutorials, and classes out there on the digital horizon.

But with each claiming to be the best, which programs are worth your time and investment? We have rounded up the top online music courses to make picking the right one easier for you:

Top 5 online music courses:

Not all musical programs are built equally. Whether you are inspired to be the next Neil Young or just want to take your jamming sessions a notch higher, there’s sure to be a program perfect for you. Below are our top picks of online music courses covering music theory, production, and learning.

1. Musical U’s musical superlearning course

Based on scientific literature and designed on techniques that have experimental proof backing them, Musical Superlearning is an online music course that delivers. Developed through a collaboration between the world-renowned music educator and guitarist Gregg Goodhart and Musical U.

The program employs superlearning techniques that we usually associate with natural musical talent. In reality, these are very much learnable skills and can be employed by anyone to learn online music courses faster and more efficiently.

The program is available to musicians of all experience levels. From those just starting to learn the ropes (or chords!), to veteran musicians looking to expand their expertise. Wherever you are on your musical journey,to avail a flat 25% discount on the course now.

click here

2. Timbaland

Timbaland is offering his first-ever master class for those interested in learning music production and beat making. The masterclass includes 15 lessons from the boss himself. Including instructions and behind-the-scenes inspiration from some of the most popular hip-hop songs we love today.

3. Udemy music courses

Udemy offers one of the vastest selections of musical courses, ranging from beginner to pro. Each course is immaculately structured and comes with detailed instructions and downloadable material. Udemy has self-paced programs catering to all budgets and musical genres.

4. Berklee College of Music

Arguably the hub of contemporary music, the top Boston musical school is now offering 12 week-long programs to music enthusiasts. The list includes an impressive 150 courses to choose from, including:

  • Music theory
  • Music Business
  • Composing
  • Songwriting
  • Music Production
  • Classical Guitar

The fee structure for the music courses online is comparatively more reasonable than their full degree programs. Musicians on the fence about buying the course can also sign up for their free trial programs.

5. Soundfly

One of the torchbearers of online musical learning, Soundfly offers an unparalleled variety of music courses online to music lovers all over the world. From courses that supplement musical careers like songwriting, production, and business, to simpler how-tos and tutorials on guitar tuning or tips on bettering your timing, Soundfly has it all.

The biggest plus is that most of the resources offered are free of charge and available to everyone.

Thanks to the internet, the world is now your oyster when it comes to learning music. Fast-track your practice sessions further by pairing your music courses online with top-of-the-line gear such as the Pulse vibrating metronome or the Core 2.

items.title: 12 best gifts for music lovers 2025
Should you learn acoustic or electric guitar? What are the differences?
items.editorNotes: The article has been rewritten for clarity and readability while preserving all factual information and required links.
items.excerptBefore: Deciding between an acoustic or electric guitar is a personal choice that varies from artist to artist. Understanding the differences will help you select the right instrument for your musical journey.
items.excerptAfter: Deciding between an acoustic or electric guitar is a personal choice. Understanding their differences can help you select the right instrument for your musical journey.
items.seoTitleBefore: Acoustic vs Electric Guitar: Which Should You Learn?
items.seoTitleAfter: Acoustic vs Electric Guitar: Which Should You Learn?
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Explore the key differences between acoustic and electric guitars to determine which instrument is best suited for your musical style and preferences.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore the key differences between acoustic and electric guitars to determine which instrument suits your musical style and preferences.
items.beforeBody:
Deciding between an acoustic or electric guitar is a personal choice that varies from artist to artist. Understanding the differences between the two will help you select the instrument that is right for you.

Choosing a guitar that resonates with your personality is essential. The right instrument can inspire you to channel your inner Jimmy Page!

What are the differences?

Both acoustic and electric guitars share more similarities than differences. You can switch your instrument later if it doesn’t suit you. The main differentiators are their sound and appearance.

Finger playability and lap feel differences

The way a guitar feels in your lap and how your fingers interact with the strings will impact your choice. These factors may seem trivial now, but they will affect your long-term comfort and relationship with music.

Acoustic and electric guitars with nylon strings have a similar level of difficulty. However, steel-string acoustic guitars are generally considered more challenging for beginners.

Acoustic guitars are portable and easy to play on the go. Electric guitars, while slimmer and lighter, require cables, an amplifier, and an electronic connection. Electric guitar strings are usually gentler on the fingers for new learners, while acoustic guitars have wooden bodies that can sustain the tension of denser strings.

Aesthetic differences

Electric guitars offer a wider variety of sizes and colors, which can be important for artists seeking an impressive instrument. Acoustic guitars may appear larger but provide better mobility. The lap feel of an acoustic guitar differs significantly from that of an electric guitar while playing.

Price differences

Pricing is another crucial factor. Electric guitars can be more expensive than acoustic guitars and often require additional accessories. Check out these affordable options that are perfect for new learners.

Tuning

Both types of guitars need to be properly adjusted and tuned for optimal performance. While they may feel different when played, the tuning process is quite similar. New learners should familiarize themselves with the basic tuning of their instruments.

Your chosen music genre

It’s advisable to learn the type of guitar that aligns with your musical style. If you envision yourself playing country songs around a campfire, an electric guitar may not be practical in that setting.

A simple rule is that if an instrument doesn’t fit your music style, learning it may not be as rewarding, regardless of its other advantages. Electric guitars provide a broader range of sounds, making lessons more engaging for younger learners. Acoustic guitars are ideal for bluegrass, classical, and country music.

Electric guitars also offer volume control, which acoustic guitars lack. This feature may make electric guitars a better choice for budding artists practicing in apartments or residential areas. You can play at a lower volume or protect your hearing with the Minuendo earplugs.

Ready to get your jam on?

Whichever type of guitar you choose, equip yourself with the right technology and tools to enhance your jamming sessions. Download the Soundbrenner app for amazing resources on the go. Remember, practice makes perfect! To elevate your guitar skills, consider investing in gadgets like Core 2 or Core Steel.

items.title: Best Hawaiian ukulele songs for beginners
items.editorNotes: The article has been rewritten for clarity and readability while maintaining the original facts and links.
items.excerptBefore: Discover the best Hawaiian ukulele songs for beginners. From iconic melodies to cultural gems, this guide has very easy-to-follow chords.
items.excerptAfter: Explore the best Hawaiian ukulele songs for beginners, featuring easy chords and beautiful melodies that make learning fun.
items.seoTitleBefore: Best Hawaiian ukulele songs for beginners
items.seoTitleAfter: Best Hawaiian Ukulele Songs for Beginners
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Discover the best Hawaiian ukulele songs for beginners. From iconic melodies to cultural gems, this guide has very easy-to-follow chords.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Discover the top Hawaiian ukulele songs perfect for beginners. Learn easy chords and enjoy beautiful melodies that will enhance your ukulele skills.
items.beforeBody:
Most instruments take months or even years of practice to be able to dash out a beautiful tune. But then there is the Ukulele, one of the easiest instruments for beginners to start their journey in the music world. All you need is a ukulele and a pocket ukulele tuner, and you can play countless Hawaiian ukulele songs. In fact, ukulele chords are much easier to learn since a typical ukulele has only four strings, and you can start playing some of the most beautiful songs with as little as three ukulele chords only!

We all have that one Hawaiian ukulele song that haunts us constantly in our dreams. Hawaiian ukulele songs are the reason many ukulele players fell in love with the instrument and chose to pick it up and learn how to dash out beautiful music. At the same time, Hawaiian music with its wide array of styles is perfect for beginners to start practicing with their ukulele. 

Now, some beginners may be wondering which Hawaiian song they should start with. Hence, today we are going to be covering some of the best Hawaiian ukulele songs for beginners to start learning, with some songs on our list having varying degrees of difficulty and different chords.

1. “Aloha Oe,” by Queen Liliuokalani

Source: Homey Hawaii

The first song on our list is “Aloha Oe” by Queen Liliuokalani with only four ukulele chords C, F, G7, and C7 and a slow tempo. This makes it an ideal song for beginners who are still learning their way around a ukulele. “Aloha Oe” has a wonderful, serene melody that makes playing the song feel almost intuitive as you follow the tune's pace with the right ukulele chords and tempo.

“Aloha Oe” is part of Hawaii's heritage as it was written by Hawaii's last monarch Queen Liliuokalani and sung by her as a farewell to Hawaii being an independent state before becoming part of the U.S.A.

2. “Ku'u home O Kahalu'u,” by Olomana

Source: SoundCloud

The second song on our list only has three ukulele chords C, F, and G7. “Ku'u home O Kahalu'u,” by Olomana is a great song for beginners with a simple yet haunting melody. This Ukulele song’s simplicity doesn’t take away anything from its beauty, with its serene tunes that carry a lot of emotional weight.

“Ku'u home O Kahalu'u,” directly translates to “My home is Kahalu'u,” as the ukulele song is about a boy who yearns for his home Kahalu'u which is a residential community on the island of Oahu. This classic Hawaiian song has a lot of different interpretations of its beautiful lyrics that reflect nostalgia and an emotional struggle. 

3. “Island Style,” by John Cruz

Source: SoundCloud

John Cruz delivers a hit for the ages with a rich story and equally melodic tunes in “Island Style,”. “Island Style,” by John Cruz, is a great song for beginners with only three ukulele chords, C, F, and G7. This iconic Hawaiian ukulele song is one of the best songs for beginners to connect emotionally with the different tunes and melodies a ukulele can produce. “Island Style,” by John Cruz, can also act as a gateway for ukulele beginners with a strong creative urge to explore different harmonies by adding their own little touches to the beautiful melody.

4. “Someone to Lava,” by Kuana Torres Kahle

Source: Scribd

“Someone to Lava” is one of the more recent additions to our list. It has three simple ukulele chords, C, G7, and F. This catchy Hawaiian ukulele song is great for beginners with a simple beat that feels intuitive as you are singing the song’s lyrics. As your ukulele knowledge increases, you can explore and learn to play some of your favorite songs. This can be done simply by checking the Ukulele tabs for that song.

“Someone to Lava” by Kuana Torres Kahle isn’t just catchy. The song’s upbeat tune and romantic lyrics create a serene atmosphere wherever they are played. The song is about a volcano praying to the earth, sea, and sky for someone to “lava” from the bottom of his magma.

5. “Mele Kalikimaka, the Hawaiian Christmas Song” by Bing Crosby

Source: Spotify

We are going to be taking it up a notch with “Mele Kalikimaka, the Hawaiian Christmas Song” by Bing Crosby. It has 5 ukulele chords, G, D7, E, C, and A7. This is the perfect Hawaiian ukulele song for beginners to learn when the holidays are just around the corner. “Mele Kalikimaka” is a song that will spread Christmas joy no matter where and when you play it.

“Mele Kalikimaka” literally means “Merry Christmas”. Robert Alex Anderson originally wrote the song in 1949. The earliest recording of the song can be traced back to 1950. Bing Crosby and the Andrews Sisters originally performed the song.

6. “Over the Rainbow,” by Israel “IZ” Kamakawiwo'ole

Source: Wikipedia

Now, we can’t make a list covering Hawaiian ukulele songs without including “Over the Rainbow”. “Over the Rainbow,” by Israel “IZ” Kamakawiwo'ole is the reason many ukulele players today choose to pick up the instrument. This song is a bit more advanced with 6 ukulele chords, C, G, Am, F, E7, and Dm7. However, it is definitely a song worth learning with serene lyrics and melody. It will transport its listeners to a much more peaceful and calming plane.

“Over the Rainbow,” by Israel Kamakawiwo'ole is a medley. It is a beautiful take on “Over the Rainbow” by actress Judy Garland and “What A Wonderful World” by Louis Armstrong. The song was originally recorded in 1988 in Hawaii.

7. “E Huli Makou,” by David Chung

The last song on our list “E Huli Makou” by David Chung is one of the most straightforward and most fun to play. It has only three ukulele chords, G7, C7 and F that you can tune using pocket ukulele chords. “E Huli Makou” is a fantastic song for ukulele beginners who love to liven the atmosphere with their music.

David Chung originally composed the song "E Huli Makou” for a Hawaiian show in 1949. He gained a bellhop, earning 26 cents an hour at the old Kona Inn. His fellow employees performed the song.

Top 5 best sheet music apps 2025
items.editorNotes: The article was rewritten for clarity and readability while maintaining the original links and factual content.
items.excerptBefore: Struggling with crumpled or lost sheet music? Discover our top picks for the best music sheet apps to simplify your practice.
items.excerptAfter: Discover the top 5 best sheet music apps for 2025 that make carrying and accessing your music easier than ever.
items.seoTitleBefore: Top 5 best sheet music apps 2025
items.seoTitleAfter: Top 5 Best Sheet Music Apps for 2025
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Struggling with crumpled or lost sheet music? Discover our top picks for the best music sheet apps to simplify your practice.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore our top picks for the best sheet music apps in 2025, featuring OnSong, MuseScore, Musicnotes, Paperless Music, and GuitarTab. Find the perfect app…
items.beforeBody:
Ever had to deal with crumpled, torn up, and even misplaced sheet music? We don’t blame you- they can be awfully troublesome to carry around. We’ve all been through the days of stuffing our sheet music into the little pockets of our instrument bags. Thankfully, new technology has given us the handy invention that is sheet music apps! Not only are your sheet music safe from wear and tear, it’s easily accessible and way more convenient to carry around.

In no particular order, here are our picks for the best sheet music apps out there right now!

1. OnSong (free with in-app subscriptions)

With countless features to support your musical workflow, OnSong allows you to store, sort, customise, and perform your music all in one place. Though the free version is sufficient, their in-app subscriptions are hard to resist. With a premium subscription, you can personalise and manage your library, edit your library across devices, email and export your sheet music, annotate, and way more.

OnSong is currently only available for download on iOS (don’t worry Android users, we’ve got more for you!)

2. MuseScore (free with in-app subscriptions)

Built by an open-source community, MuseScore is an impressive sheet music software. You’ll have access to over 1.5 million pieces of free sheet music for most instruments under various genres. With their in-app subscription, MusicScore PRO+, you’ll have access to an ad-free MusicScore, more learning and sharing options, and more.

One of the most versatile software for musicians, MuseScore is available on Windows, Mac, Linux, iOS, and Android.

3. Musicnotes (free with in-app subscriptions)

With over 400,000 sheet music arrangements readily available, Musicnotes is great for beginners to long-time musicians alike. Although you’ll have to individually purchase each sheet music you’d like, Musicnotes also allows you to import your own sheet music app via Dropbox. In fact, being able to individually purchase the compositions you like rather than paying for a bunch of sheets you’ll never touch, may be a good thing! With over 50 million downloads, Musicnotes is definitely a force to be reckoned with.

Another sheet music app that’s compatible with many devices, Musicnotes is available on Windows, Mac, Linux, iOS, and Android.

4. Paperless Music ($4.99)

The most budget-friendly app in this list, Paperless Music has no ads, no in-app purchases, no subscriptions, and no requirement for an account. With a one and done cost of $4.99, Paperless Music is a straight-to-the-point, general-purpose music reader with the ability to import and export your own sheet music. They also have some cool features. For example, their Collection feature that allows you to group the sheet music apps of your choice together for convenience. They also have editing tools which allow you to make annotations!

Unfortunately, Paperless Music is currently only available for iOS.

5. GuitarTab (free with in-app purchases)

Another straight-to-the-point sheet music app, GuitarTab is a sheet music app optimized for guitarists. With access to more than 800,000 free tabs, GuitarTab is great for users who want to learn a song easily and simply. GuitarTab is also relatively budget-friendly in comparison to many other guitar-oriented sheet music apps. There’s definitely a reason why GuitarTab has received tens of thousands of downloads and many positive reviews.

GuitarTab is available on both iOS and Android.

Even with sheet music apps that have auto-scrolling features for your hands-free convenience, sometimes you might just want to flip through the pages yourself. All the apps featured in this list are compatible with the Stomp Bluetooth foot pedal- the most robust and reliable pedal in the market. Flip through your sheet music hands-free with the Stomp Bluetooth foot pedal!

items.title: 16 best Valentine's Day gifts for musicians 2025
How to practice with the Soundbrenner Core 2
items.editorNotes: Revised for clarity and readability while maintaining factual content.
items.excerptBefore: We sometimes get asked by musicians what’s the best way to practice with their Soundbrenner Core 2. Here's an article on how to navigate.
items.excerptAfter: Learn how to effectively practice with the Soundbrenner Core 2 through warm-up exercises, tracking progress, and using a metronome.
items.seoTitleAfter: How to Practice with the Soundbrenner Core 2
items.seoDescriptionBefore: We sometimes get asked by musicians what’s the best way to practice with their Soundbrenner Core 2. Here's an article on how to navigate.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Discover step-by-step instructions for practicing with the Soundbrenner Core 2, including warm-up techniques, tracking progress, and using a metronome.
items.beforeBody:
We sometimes get asked by musicians what’s the best way to practice with their Soundbrenner Core 2. In this article, we will give you step-by-step instructions on how to navigate your Core 2 to start practicing with it.

If you haven’t checked out one of our introductory videos just yet, we highly recommend doing so. Watch Soundbrenner instructor Nate Wong unbox a Soundbrenner Core Steel. Core 2 and Core Steel Unboxing

Warm-up and prepare

Warming up before practicing your music piece is important. Why? Because warming up reinforces your muscle memories, making your fingers or vocal cords more comfortable when you start to play. Also, this helps to prevent injuries, especially before an intensive practice session.

You can easily find warm-up exercises online to trigger your muscle memory. Practice melodic patterns or changes, long and short phrases, and fast and slow tempos.

Here is an example:

 

To warm up, you can start playing the scales with different tempos: 80 BPM, 90 BPM, 100 BPM, 110 BPM, 120 BPM. Here is how you can set this up with the Soundbrenner Core 2:

Set up a standard 4/4 time signature and set the BPM to 80. Save this rhythm to your setlist. 

Repeat step 1 with 90, 100, 110, and 120 BPM.

Start practicing your scales with this setlist. Run through each tempo twice or until you feel like you can play smoothly.

Layer your warm-up by playing your scales in legato and staccato. Legato is a fluid, continuous sound between notes. Staccato is performed with each note sharply detached or separated from the others. Legato and staccato might sound different for each instrument because of timbre, the tunings, and the range. There are techniques that you can follow. Here is a perfect differentiation between the two sounds with the piano.

If you play a string instrument, make sure it is tuned with the Soundbrenner Core 2’s handy contact tuner feature! Simply twist the Core 2 off its rig, snap it onto your instrument, and start tuning. How convenient!

Tracking your practice

Charting your improvement is important. If you cannot do your G major scale in 120 BPM, it’s alright to just keep practicing at a slower BPM, and eventually, you’ll be able to play your scale or riffs at 120 BPM.

Practicing your piece with a metronome

When learning a new piece, we sometimes disregard the tempo and the time signature, but it is these components that set the vital organizational direction and mood of the song. You can check out this video by David Bennett Piano giving real examples of how a change in tempo affects a song. A stable tempo and accents in the right place play a huge role in creating the shape and backbone of the song.

Although most pieces would have BPM at the very top of the piece. Depending on your preference, you can either bring it up or bring it down a couple of BPM to what sounds smooth and right for you. 

Once you find the tempo that you would like to achieve, say 136 BPM, you can start practicing by halving that BPM to make sure that you are hitting the right notes with the right technique.

Start playing at 68 BPM which is half of 136, and then build it up to 102 BPM which is ¾ the speed, and then finally 136 BPM. This slowly allows you to understand the finger techniques and challenge your muscle memory. 

With Soundbrenner Core 2, you can easily change the tempo by turning the wheel. Also, you can set accents to help indicate when you should place more emphasis on notes.

Solidify your rhythm

If you are practicing a song that is usually played with a band you can find a drum loop online with the tempo you are practicing with and tap in the beat to your watch as you start playing the drum loop. 

If you are practicing with your band and they also own a Soundbrenner Pulse or Core 2 device, you can easily sync up all your devices to practice together! Here’s how. 

Here are just some of our tips on practicing with your Soundbrenner Core 2. We hope this makes your practice experience a lot more effective and enjoyable.

With the Soundbrenner Core 2, you can easily adjust the tempo by turning the wheel. You can also set accents to indicate when to emphasize certain notes.

Solidify your rhythm

If you are practicing a song typically played with a band, you can find a drum loop online that matches your practice tempo. Tap the beat on your watch as you play along with the drum loop.

If you are practicing with a band and they also have a Soundbrenner Pulse or Core 2 device, you can easily sync all your devices for a collaborative practice session! Here’s how.

These are just a few tips for practicing with your Soundbrenner Core 2. We hope this enhances your practice experience, making it more effective and enjoyable.

items.title: 6 reasons why it’s not too late to learn a music instrument
items.editorNotes: The article was rewritten for clarity and readability while maintaining the original content and links.
items.excerptBefore: Be it a new year’s resolution, or a lifelong dream in your bucket list —  it’s never too late to start learning a music instrument.
items.excerptAfter: Discover why it's never too late to learn a musical instrument, from enhancing memory to improving wellbeing.
items.seoTitleBefore: 6 reasons why it’s not too late to learn a music instrument
items.seoTitleAfter: 6 Reasons Why It's Never Too Late to Learn a Musical…
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Be it a new year’s resolution, or a lifelong dream in your bucket list — it’s never too late to start learning a music instrument.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore the benefits of learning a musical instrument at any age, including memory enhancement, wellbeing, and access to online resources.
items.beforeBody:
Have you been considering piano lessons? Or a self-learning journey with a relative’s old guitar? Be it a new year’s resolution to pick up a new hobby, or a lifelong dream in your bucket list — we’re here to tell you why it’s never too late to start to learn a music instrument. 

1. Music enhances your memory

A study by Stanford has shown that learning a music instrument can stimulate areas of your brain that are involved with focusing, predicting, and updating memories. Over longer periods of time, music engages your brain and contributes to how your brain polishes up on its ability to retain information. Another study involving adults aged 60 to 85 (with no musical experience!) showed how a few weeks of music lessons improved their verbal fluency and processing speed.

Though it’s no sure solution, there’s no denying all this research showing how constant exposure to music can enhance your memory!

2. The connection between music and your wellbeing

Throughout adulthood, responsibilities with your career or family may restrict you. Only until after you find yourself settled down might you finally consider doing something for yourself. Learning something new out of your own personal interest contributes to a sense of meaning in the world. Like belongingness, participation, and involvement. 

Numerous studies have researched and proven the positive effects of art education on individual wellbeing, most of these finding that music-making in all its forms and participation contributes positively to one’s happiness. This makes learning a music instrument quite literally the act of chasing happiness. 

3. It's hard work, but it's not impossible to learn a music instrument

We believe that learning a music instrument is like learning a new language. Something that is effectively taught to be easier done during one’s childhood. There’s no denying that anything picked up during your formative years will often be easier retained. However, like the saying goes, “Rome wasn’t built in a day”, hard work applies to everyone for every skill, no matter what age you’re at!

Making a move, no matter when you do it, is the first step to mastering a music instrument. There is no better time than now!

4. Online resources are just one click away

We’re blessed to be living in a technologically advanced where learning a new skill is just a Google search away. 

The Soundbrenner Blog is one hub you might want to consider getting started on. Looking for online resources to learn music instrument theory? We’ve got it. Don’t know how to structure your practice sessions? Got you covered! Need inspiration to get started on your self-learning journey? We’re here to help.

If you’re a visual learner, you may want to check out Soundbrenner Learn on the Metronome by Soundbrenner app. Featuring professional instructors from different musical backgrounds is a must. You can learn just about everything from odd meters to life-changing practice of music instrument techniques. 

The Internet is abundant with millions of resources waiting for you to try out! You may even find that you enjoy the process of searching for educational sites as much as the learning itself.

5. Technological advancements have greatly enhanced the learning process

Combining online resources with innovative tools can significantly assist your learning process. 

The Soundbrenner Pulse is a great place to start if you want a simple but smart device to help you. When you first start learning a music instrument, the Pulse can help you with building and strengthening your sense of rhythm through internalizing the beat. Even after you’ve passed the beginning stages of mastering rhythm, the Pulse can be used during practice sessions and gigs to support you with feeling the beat. 

Innovative music-tech companies are on the rise, so take advantage of this and supplement your learning sessions with useful tools.

6. You only live once

As cliché as it is, you really do only live once. Whether you’ve just reached adulthood or have been gracefully aging for some time now, the opportunity to learn will always be waiting for you. If you have the resources to start to learn a music instrument, why not start now? 

If all you’re waiting for is a sign to get started, here it is — Your life is yours to live, so go and learn the piano! Take those violin lessons! Pick up that guitar! And always remember that every master was once a beginner.

items.title: How to improve your rhythm with a metronome
items.editorNotes: The article has been rewritten for clarity and readability while preserving all factual information and required links/images.
items.excerptBefore: Timing is a real key thing in music. Here are our tips to improve your rhythm with a metronome and preventing it from being sloppy.
items.excerptAfter: Discover how practicing with a metronome can enhance your rhythm, improve your timing, and track your progress as a musician.
items.seoTitleBefore: How to improve your rhythm with a metronome
items.seoTitleAfter: Improve Your Rhythm with a Metronome
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Timing is a real key thing in music. Here are our tips to improve your rhythm with a metronome and preventing it from being sloppy.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Learn how to enhance your rhythm and timing with a metronome. Discover different types of metronomes and effective practice techniques.
items.beforeBody:
Timing is a real key thing in music. You can get away with some sloppy chords, but you can’t really get away with your rhythm being sloppy.

Although some movement and flexibility add personality to the piece, when you’re being a bit too flexible with the tempo, especially when it’s something new to you, it will cause the whole piece to fall apart. A feeling of the pulse can help to get the piece back into shape.

Most musicians know that metronomes are a very useful tool to help you keep time. This is particularly helpful for beginners, who often find they tend to rush a little bit.

Why you should practice with a metronome

1. It enhances your internal sense of rhythm

A common rhythmic problem faced by many beginners is a lack of awareness of the pulse when playing. Practicing with a metronome will help improve rhythm, and it will help you pay attention to where the pulse is, and how all the notes fit into each beat.

2. It helps you play faster (or slower)

To train yourself to be able to cope with playing faster, you have to gradually build up your speed. A metronome can help you to increase your speed in small increments. On the flip side, a metronome can help you practice slower too, which is necessary for building better technique.

3. It helps you keep track of progress

When you practice with a metronome, you know your rate of improvement exactly. This can be very motivating as you have evidence of how far your practice has brought you!

How to practice with a metronome

Choose a practice speed that stays in relation to the final tempo that you would like to play in. Say that the final tempo is 138 BPM. Next, set up your metronome and practice your piece at half the speed, which is 69 BPM. Then ⅔ tempo, 92 BPM. Next, at ¾ of the final tempo, which is 104 BPM. Then increase it to ⅞ of the final tempo. And finally, play at the final tempo. This puts everything into a structure and helps you to get a really strong feeling of the pulse.

This is just one of the many ways to practice with a metronome. Try out different methods to find what works best for you! Metronomes improve rhythm to a great extent.

Types of metronomes

1. Classic pendulum metronome

A more traditional option, these can look quite stylish in a vintage way and are relatively affordable. However, they don’t have many features on top of adjusting the speed on the meter and are not easily portable. Some musicians find that the tick from this classic device works very well for them, while others are less so.

2. Digital metronomes

Compact and more features available, easy to customize, but it’s easy to overlook and musicians may often find that they've left it at home just when they need it. You can get this metronome from Amazon.

3. Metronome apps

Perhaps the most popular option these days due to its convenience, metronome apps usually come with lots of customization options as well. Soundbrenner has its own free metronome app, which you can download here for iOS and Android. You can save your metronome presets into set lists, customize the time signature, and subdivision, pick the metronome sound you prefer, and more!

4. Wearable vibrating metronome

Soundbrenner Pulse, a wearable vibrating metronome

Yes, these exist! The Soundbrenner Pulse is the only wearable metronome in the market that signals the pulse through vibrations, helping you to improve rhythm. Soundbrenner's latest wearable, the Soundbrenner Core 2, offers the same technology in a smaller, sleeker body, and has the added features of a contact tuner, dB meter, and watch all-in-one device. Since you wear it like you’d wear a watch, you’ll always have it with you whenever you feel like practicing!

Soundbrenner Core 2 Steel, the watch for musicians

Learning to play along with the metronome and make it into something that you’re comfortable doing, is an important skill. The more you practice, the more you’d see its benefit over time.

Happy practicing!

items.seoDescriptionBefore: Looking for the 10 best gifts for ukulele players in 2025? Check out these unique and thoughtful presents for all ukulele enthusiasts.

items.beforeBody:
If you are looking for presents for passionate ukulele players, you're in luck! The world of ukulele accessories and gadgets is continuously evolving. Whether they are a seasoned ukulele player or just starting out, these ten handpicked gifts are sure to bring a smile to their faces. 

Let's dive into the 10 best gifts for ukulele players in 2025!

1. Soundbrenner Pulse 

Our top pick for the best gift is the Soundbrenner Pulse, a wearable metronome that provides precise vibrations to help ukulele players stay in perfect rhythm. It comes with a custom vibration motor 7 times stronger than a smartphone and a mobile app to regulate all its functions. 

With this device at hand, they’ll never miss a beat again. This innovative device will enhance their performance, making it a must-have for any serious player.

 

 



View at Soundbrenner





2. Hannabach ukulele strings

Every ukulele player knows the importance of having fresh strings. Gift them a set of these high-quality Hannabach ukulele strings by Strings and Beyond. These are tailored to their gauge requirements. It will not only improve the sound but also encourage them to play more often.



 

View at Strings and Beyond


3. Personalized ukulele leather strap

A stylish and comfortable ukulele strap is a practical gift. It allows players to perform standing up without any discomfort. Consider getting a custom leather strap to add a unique touch to their playing experience.

 

View at Etsy






4. Zither wooden ukulele stand 

A reliable ukulele stand keeps the instrument safe and easily accessible. This sturdy and compact wooden ukulele stand can accommodate different ukulele sizes, making it convenient for home practice and gigs.

 

View at The Uke Bug






5. RocketMusic RT40U digital clip-on ukulele tuner

An accurate and easy-to-use clip-on tuner is an essential tool for any ukulele player. This high-quality ukulele tuner will help them keep their instrument in tune, ensuring a pleasant and harmonious playing experience.

View at Amazon




6. Songbook of popular ukulele songs 

Consider gifting your favorite ukulele player, this collection of popular ukulele songs created by Lindsay Muller. This songbook is sure to add to their collection and keep them inspired and motivated.

 

View at Lindsay Muller Store




7. Ukulele cleaning and maintenance kit

Help your ukulele enthusiast keep their instrument in top-notch condition with this amazing and pocket-friendly ukulele cleaning and maintenance kit. This thoughtful gift will ensure their ukulele stays clean, polished, and ready to play.

 

View at Music Nomad




8. Handcrafted customized wooden ukulele picks 

Upgrade their playing experience with these personalized and handcrafted ukulele picks made from wood. These picks can significantly impact the sound and add a touch of elegance to their playing style.

View at Etsy




9. Portable music stand

For ukulele players who enjoy reading music or practicing with notes, portable music stands like this one is a practical and thoughtful gift. Such a lightweight and foldable stand provides ease of transportation and increases convenience.

 

View at Amazon

10. Core steel musicians smartwatch

This sleek and stylish smartwatch not only tracks time but also features a vibrating metronome, tuner, and decibel meter. A perfect gadget for ukulele players and musicians. 

With its latest technology and premium design, it's an ultimate gift, that will elevate their musical experience to new heights.

 

View at Soundbrenner


 

Briefly, this list of the 10 best gifts for ukulele players in 2025 is carefully curated to cater to the various needs of ukulele players. From practical accessories to unique experiences, these presents are sure to make their ukulele journey even more fun. 

Happy gifting!

items.title: Painful events that shaped music
items.editorNotes: Ensure all links and images are preserved as required.
items.excerptBefore: We will briefly explore music that has been inspired by pain, and how we believe suffering is sometimes a necessary component of music.
items.excerptAfter: Explore how painful events have shaped music throughout history, from pandemics to heartbreak and social movements.
items.seoTitleBefore: Painful events that shaped music
items.seoTitleAfter: Painful Events That Shaped Music
items.seoDescriptionBefore: We will briefly explore music that has been inspired by pain, and how we believe suffering is sometimes a necessary component of music.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Discover how pain and suffering have influenced music across different eras, from the Spanish flu to the Civil Rights Movement.
items.beforeBody:
The Connection Between Pain and Music

As Albert Einstein said "creativity is intelligence having fun", which is one of the core identities that Soundbrenner strives to keep. We are featuring a few fun pieces on the topic of pop culture and music composition - exploring why music is the way it is. In this first entry, we will briefly explore music that has been inspired by pain. Here's how we believe suffering is sometimes a necessary component of music.

Pandemic Blues

Humans have always tried to make sense of confusing and troubling times through the arts, particularly through pain and music. It's what separates us from our animal counterparts, gives us hope and represents shifts in world history. Whether it be the emancipation of slavery or a global pandemic.

Pandemic may seem like a buzzed word as of now. However, back in 1918, the Spanish Influenza shook the world, killing between 20-50 million victims worldwide. The song “Influenza 1918” by Essie Jenkins, paints a bleak picture of the effect of “Spanish flu” regardless of class with “kill you rich or poor”. It uses blues and an English hymn style of composition for its arrangement.

Another notable example of music that has been inspired by pain is “Tainted Love” cover by Soft Cell. It took on a whole new meaning during the AIDS and HIV crisis compared to its 1964 original by Gloria Jones. Most recently, although not all that highbrow, a remix by iMarkkeyz takes a sample from Cardi B saying “CORONAVIRUS!”. This became a viral song, giving people a well needed - albeit comical relief from whichever lockdown they were experiencing. Through these songs, we see how pain and music intertwine to help us cope with difficult times.

War (What is it good for)

So what about other types of collective trauma?
No matter what side you were on, hundreds of songs were written to either boost morale and quell homesickness. Think "We’ll meet again” - Vera Lynn written during WW2. Trying to make sense of the horrors they’ve witnessed through music can be cathartic for both the listener and the musician.

Heartbreak and its influence

Heartbreak, perhaps the most relatable form of pain, has inspired a mass of artists. Several artists have even made it their whole brand. I’m not naming names…Adele. “Greensleeves'' is an old example of a 16th century folk song about love and loss-a song. It was supposedly written by the infamous Tudor King, Henry VIII after being repeatedly rejected by Anne Boyln. Being heartbroken or in pain alone has influenced some of the most powerful music. So, that’s one good thing that comes out of it.

The legacy of slavery and the Civil Rights Movement

Slavery and then the black rights movement have birthed a multitude of genres that have been fused, mixed and even stolen, to create a rich tapestry of sounds we enjoy so much today. “Oh Freedom” (1865), an anthem of the Civil Rights Movement and made popular by human rights activist and singer Odetta, is one of many examples. The composer and writer of the song remains unknown. Despite the lyrical context of this song, it has the ability to communicate its ideas in a transcendent way. My personal favorite is “A change is gonna come” by Sam Cooke.

The universality of pain and music

So, next time there’s an event that shakes the world or even a heartbroken teen from a small unimportant town, know that not far behind any of these events, an artist will be brewing a song in the background. A song that captures the sounds of the moment, where pain and music intertwine to form a poignant narrative. This song will channel the raw emotion of the experience, weaving together the threads of anguish and hope into melodies that resonate deeply. It will be given to the world so we feel less alone, and more understood, as the harmony of pain and music creates a universal language of empathy and connection.

Check out different artists and musicians that we feature on our Instagram.

items.excerptBefore: Discover the best beach instruments to enhance your seaside experience! From breezy ukuleles to lively hand pans, we've got you covered.
items.excerptAfter: Discover the top 5 musical instruments perfect for enhancing your beach experience. From ukuleles to bongo drums, make your seaside days more enjoyable!
items.seoTitleBefore: Top 5 instruments you can easily take to the beach
items.seoTitleAfter: Top 5 Instruments to Take to the Beach
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Discover the best beach instruments to enhance your seaside experience! From breezy ukuleles to lively hand pans, we've got you covered.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore the best musical instruments for the beach, including ukuleles, handpans, cajons, acoustic guitars, and bongo drums. Make your beach days…
items.beforeBody:
If you're planning a sun-soaked day at the beach, why not add some sweet melodies to the mix? Music and the beach go hand in hand, and having the right beach instruments can elevate your seaside experience to a whole new level of fun and relaxation.

At Soundbrenner, we’re experts in everything music-related, and here’s our list of the top 5 musical instruments that are perfect for the beach, making your beach days even more enjoyable. 

So, grab your sunscreen, and let's dive in!

1. Ukulele

The ukulele is a lightweight instrument that is perfect for the beach. Its soft, mellow tones evoke the feeling of a tropical paradise and instantly transport you to a carefree state of mind. 

Whether you're playing solo or jamming with friends around a bonfire, the ukulele brings a sense of warmth and happiness to any beach gathering.

Buy Now

2. Handpan

If you want to add a light and mesmerizing touch to your beach experience, a handpan is a must-have. This unique instrument produces soothing, celestial sounds that blend harmoniously with the sound of the ocean waves. 

Made from steel, the handpan is durable and resistant to the beach elements. Whether you're an experienced player or a beginner, playing the handpan at the beach, will undoubtedly create a magical ambience that everyone will love.

Buy Now

3. Cajon

For those who love the rhythm and want to feel the beat of the beach, the Cajon is the ideal beach instrument to take along. 

The Cajon is a box-shaped drum that you sit on and play with your hands. It produces a rich and melodious sound that can easily set the tone for a lively beach party. The Cajon's versatility makes it a favorite among beach musicians.

Buy Now

4. Acoustic guitar

A classic choice for any outdoor setting, the acoustic guitar is a staple at the beach. Its melodious sound resonates beautifully in the open air. You can play anything from soulful ballads to upbeat summer anthems. 

Whether you're strumming under a palm tree or by a beachside bonfire, the acoustic guitar brings a sense of familiarity and joy to your beach escapades.

Buy Now

5. Bongo drums

Bongo drum, is a fantastic beach instrument for adding a tribal and earthy vibe to your beach gatherings. It's a hand-held drum with a simple design, making it easy to play for all skill levels. 

The bongo drums’ rhythmic beats can create a captivating atmosphere. It is perfect for dancing along with the waves or enjoying a meditative moment by the shore.

Buy Now



Bringing musical instruments with you can truly enhance the overall beach experience, creating lasting memories and joyous moments with friends and family. The ukulele, handpan, cajon, acoustic guitar, and bongo drums are all fantastic choices that cater to different musical preferences and skill levels. 

So, the next time you head to the beach, don't forget to pack your favorite beach instrument and make your beach days more melodious and unforgettable.

Let the harmonies flow and the good times roll! 🎵

items.title: How to develop a solid rhythm foundation and rock the beat
5 best tuner apps in 2025 | Keep your groove in tune
items.editorNotes: Revised for clarity and readability while preserving all factual information and required links/images.
items.excerptBefore: For all musicians out there, from budding beginners to seasoned maestros, we've got the perfect list for you: the top 5 best tuner apps!
items.excerptAfter: Discover the top 5 tuner apps of 2025 that will keep your musical groove in tune. From Soundbrenner to Pano Tuner, find the perfect app for your needs.
items.seoTitleAfter: 5 Best Tuner Apps in 2025 | Keep Your Groove in Tune
items.seoDescriptionBefore: For all musicians out there, from budding beginners to seasoned maestros, we've got the perfect list for you: the top 5 best tuner apps!
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore the top 5 tuner apps of 2025 that every musician should have. From metronomes to professional-grade tuners, find the best app for your musical…
items.beforeBody:
You know what they say — “You gotta stay in tune to keep your groove!”. Alright, I made that up. But hey, let's dive into the world of musical precision. For all you musicians out there, from budding beginners to seasoned maestros, we've got the perfect list for you: the top 5 best tuner apps in 2025. Say goodbye to clunky hardware tuners, and say hello to smartphone efficiency.

Let's rock and roll, folks!

Why should I download a tuner app? 

If you're asking this, you're probably not a trombonist who's tired of that pesky sharp F above middle C. But that's okay, let's break it down. Each instrument has its own personality, with some notes naturally out of tune. A tuner app will help you decipher these mysteries, saving your bandmates from out-of-tune agony.

In the past, tuners were separate devices that you had to lug around (along with your instrument). But today, we're in the era of smartphones! You can get a high-quality tuner app that's often cheaper than the hardware tuner and comes with fun extra features, like metronomes and recording options. Talk about hitting two birds with one stone! 

So, without further ado, let's jump into the list.

The top 5 tuner apps of 2025

Before we start, remember that using a tuner app is as simple as ABC. Download the app, open it, and let the app do its magic. It'll use your device's microphone, identify the pitch, and let you know how in tune you are. Piece of cake, right? Now let's get into the specifics of our top picks.

1. The Metronome by Soundbrenner

The Metronome by Soundbrenner is your one-stop shop for all things musical. Not only does it provide a chromatic tuner, but it also offers a metronome, practice tracking abilities, set list libraries, etc. And, with its accuracy down to 1/100th of a cent, you'll never have to worry about being out of tune again.

Additionally, you could pair this with the Soundbrenner Core 2 or Soundbrenner Pulse seamlessly and take your practice/performance to the next level.

IOS Android

2. TonalEnergy

Known as the "Swiss Army Knife" of tuner apps, TonalEnergy offers a tuner, metronome, sustained tone (drone), and recording features. And the best part? You can use them all at the same time! TonalEnergy is a treasure for musicians looking to perfect their intonation while having a record to review later. Oh, and did I mention the smiley face for positive reinforcement when you're in tune? Isn't that adorable?

IOS Android

3. iStroboSoft

The big guns of the tuning world, strobe tuners, have found their way to your phone with iStroboSoft. Known for accuracy, this app brings a professional-grade tuner to your smartphone. Yes, it's a bit pricier, but for the musician who's all about precision, it's worth every penny.

IOS

4. BOSS Tuner

Old school meets new school with the BOSS Tuner app. BOSS has been a long-time favorite among musicians for their reliable hardware tuners. Now, they're bringing their trusty technology to the digital world with a free app that looks and feels like their traditional tuners.

IOSAndroid

5. Pano Tuner

Looking for a ready-to-go tuner right after installation? Pano Tuner is your best bet. It's free, reliable, and gets you in tune with a jiffy.

IOSAndroid

Overview of the Top 5 best tuner apps

Here's a handy table summarizing our top 5:

App Platforms Price
The Metronome by Soundbrenner Apple and Android Free
iStroboSoft Apple $9.99
Tunable Apple and Android $3.99
BOSS Tuner Apple and Android Free
Pano Tuner Apple and Android Free

Honorable mentions

Don't forget to check out the Cleartune and Pro Guitar Tuning apps. Cleartune has had its ups and downs, but some folks still swear by it. Pro Guitar Tuner, despite its name, works with many acoustical instruments, not just guitars.

Let’s get in tune

Whether you're belting out a classic tune or strumming away on your guitar, the difference between a good performance and a great one often comes down to staying in tune. So, give one (or all) of these apps a try, and you'll see the difference they can make in your musical journey. 

Happy tuning!

items.beforeBody:
AI music generators are a concept that has been introduced previously for musicians of all genres.

From auto-tune to making practice sessions more effective, musicians have been using tech to push the boundaries of what we consider music. But now, with the rise of AI music generators, technology is taking the reins and leading us to new frontiers in the realm of music. Read on to explore our top picks for the best AI music generators out there:

Rocking with Robots: 5 AI music generators that actually sound great

As the music industry evolves, technology has become a driving force in shaping almost every sound we hear. The robots are now composing the tunes, and the possibilities seem endless! Let's explore the top 5 AI music generators out there and discuss how they are changing the game in the music industry:

1. Amper Music

Amper Music’s cloud-based platform is user-friendly and versatile. Are you looking to change the tune of any existing piece of music to fit the mood better? Or want to generate background music or soundtracks quickly? When it comes to rapidly generating original tracks, it's hard to beat this AI-backed music software.

Check it out!

2. AIVA AI Music Generator

This one has AI right in its name! Developed by Amper Music, this music composition software uses machine learning (ML) algorithms to create original royalty-free tracks. Perfect for producing a wide range of music, including pop, rock, and classical. This platform also lets users set mood, tempo, and instrumentation preferences.

The AIVA AI Music Generator is trained on a vast dataset of musical patterns and styles. Thanks to its high-quality output and easy integration, it's the perfect companion for musicians looking to create professional-grade music.

Check it out!

3. Amadeus Code

This platform combines an AI engine using chord progressions from some of the most well-known songs in the world. Using Amadeus Code’s AI music generator, users can generate original music by selecting any genre, mood, and length for the music.

The AI-powered platform then analyzes these selected parameters and generates a new musical composition in real-time. Another impressive feature is the Amadeus Code mobile app that allows users to create and listen to music on the go.

Check it out!

4. Melodrive

Melodrive’s easy-to-use software uses machine learning algorithms to generate original music compositions. The AI engine analyzes each user's specific input, such as mood, tempo, and key signature, to create music in real time. In addition, this platform boasts a host of tools for musicians to customize the generated music.

Highly recommended for new and veteran musicians looking to explore the capabilities of AI technology in music.

Check it out!

5. HumTap

Do you want to get all the advantages AI offers, but are a hard-core traditionalist at heart? Then HumTap AI music generator for you. You can hum or sing to this AI-powered software, and the algorithm will take over from there.

This platform is the all-in-one solution that lets you add in vocals later, tweak the composition to perfection, and even make videos.

Check it out!

Musical Mastermind Machines: We’ve short-listed the best AI music generators to try.

Press play on your musical future today

AI music generators are here to stay. So, it's time to turn up the volume and see what these AI music generators have in store for us—interested to learn more? Head over to Soundbrenner now to check out the latest in musical tech and gear.

items.title: 5 best ways to upgrade/modify your guitar
items.editorNotes: The article was rewritten for clarity and readability while preserving all factual information and required links/images.
items.excerptBefore: Don't toss that old guitar out just yet, you can still make use of it! Here are the top 5 best ways to upgrade/modify your guitar.
items.excerptAfter: Discover the top 5 ways to upgrade or modify your guitar for better sound and personalization. From new pickups to changing strings, enhance your musical experience.
items.seoTitleBefore: 5 best ways to upgrade/modify your guitar
items.seoTitleAfter: 5 Best Ways to Upgrade or Modify Your Guitar
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Don't toss that old guitar out just yet, you can still make use of it! Here are the top 5 best ways to upgrade/modify your guitar.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore the best methods to upgrade or modify your guitar, including new pickups, strings, and more. Personalize your sound and improve your playing…
items.beforeBody:
Let’s face it; good guitars are expensive! Even if you buy something from the more reasonably priced end of the spectrum. Check out these fab Guitar options that won't break the bank while you’re at it! At Soundbrenner, we’re nothing if not creative! That’s why we've compiled a list of the 5 best ways to upgrade/modify your guitar. 

5 best ways to modify your guitar/modify and shake things up!

Considering your sizeable investment in your guitar, upgrading your gear to make it your own instead of paying top dollar for a brand-new one makes more sense. Read on to see our top 5 best ways to upgrade/modify your guitar ideas:

1. Get yourself the Soundbrenner Core 2 Pronto 

Why did the guitarist refuse to wear a watch? Because he preferred to keep time with his fingers! That was before he knew about the Soundbrenner Core 2! 

Why settle for just one level up your guitar when the Soundbrenner Core 2 can give it 7+ instant upgrades? It's a contact tuner, a vibrating metronome, a decibel meter, a timer, a stopwatch, and a regular ‘ol smartwatch all rolled into one. This gadget is designed with a guitarist like you in mind to help you improve your solo performances and your brand practices by linking up to five devices at once.

We recommend getting yourself (and your guitar) the Core 2 if you want to make just one upgrade.

Buy now

2. New pickups

One of the easiest, instant, and best ways to upgrade your guitar is getting yourself some new pickups. You can go as high-end or economical as you want with guitar pickups. It's crucial to try out a few different materials and brands before you find the option best for your guitar, so don't be afraid to experiment here. 

These Fender Original and Seymour Duncan pickups are excellent options for levelling up your pickups.

Buy now

3. String her up!

The soul of your guitar is in its strings. Changing the type of strings on your gear can often give you that “oomph” effect you might be missing, both sound-wise and looks-wise. D’Addario, Elixir, Ernie Ball, and Dunlop are well-known guitar string brands for all types of coated and uncoated guitar string options. 

Bonus: updating your guitar strings can also be great for practice-sore hands and fingers!

4. Tuner knobs and buttons 

Tuner knobs are another excellent and simple modification for your equipment that would improve your guitar's sound and aesthetics. Make sure you know the right size of tuner buttons according to your guitar type before getting a new set if you want to treat your guitar with a bit of TLC.

5. Modify the Bridge

The bridge of your guitar supports the strings and transmits vibrations to make your music sound unique. That's why modifications to the bridge or even getting a new set of bridge pins can make a drastic difference in sound and tune.  

Show it some love by switching out the run-of-the-mill bridge, your guitar probably came with a better quality one of the same model and type. But first, ensure you understand the working of your current bridge and check if it’s the fixed or floating type.

Buy now

items.seoDescriptionBefore: It's Mother's Day! Get ready to rock your mom’s world this Mother’s Day with our top-rated 7 Mother’s Day gifts!
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore our top 7 musical Mother's Day gifts for 2025 that will make your mom feel special and appreciated. Perfect for any music-loving mom!
items.beforeBody:
Get ready to rock your mom’s world this Mother’s Day with our top-rated 7 Mother's Day gifts.

Are you tired of giving your mom the same old, boring Mother's Day gifts? She won't ever admit to it, but so is she! This year, why not mix it up with some musical flair? From shower singing to car karaoke, we've got you covered with the top 7 melodic ideas for Mother's Day gifts sure to make mom feel like a rock star (or at least a shower star).



Make your mom’s heart sing with these 7 Mother’s Day gifts:

Showing Mom how much you love her, especially that one special day of the year, can get monotonous. That’s why we've compiled this list of sound-sational Mother's Day Gift ideas to help show how much you care about her and her musical talents!


1. Soundbrenner Core 2

The Soundbrenner Core 2 is taking the musical world by storm thanks to its wide range of versatile features. It’s aesthetically pleasing and compact enough to be worn daily (additional brownie points!). A contact tuner, dB meter, and vibrating metronome all rolled into one sleek package. It can also be worn for a hands-free practice session and is whisper-quiet.

 

Buy now


2. Personalized soundwave

Is mom’s rhythm and timing already near-perfect? If that’s the case with your mom, we have the perfect Mother’s Day gift for her. Order her this customized soundwave that comes printed on an elegant silk background. It can be personalized with her favorite song and her favorite metallic color. Or, whichever color scheme goes best with her interiors, you choose!

 

Buy now


3. Minuendo earplugs


The Minuendo Lossless Earplugs make the perfect Mother’s Day gift for all moms with a musical streak. 

Mom shouldn't have to compromise on sound quality, just like she never compromised on always giving you the best. Besides, stepless adjustment and natural, no-loss sound are just some features that make these the perfect Mother’s Day gift option for all the supermoms out there. Car karaoke, here she comes!

 

Buy now


4. Playtron


The perfect way for your mum to express herself through her music and have fun at the same time! Playtron MIDI Controller offers a unique way to create your own custom MIDI controller. It has the ability to connect up to 16 objects with alligator clips. This allows you to use it with several mobile apps, online synthesizers, or your favorite DAW.

From fruits to plants, and even toys, the possibilities are endless. Connect the clips from objects capable of conducting electricity to Playtron, and assign a different MIDI note (C1 to D#2 by default) to each object. Then, you can start playing and creating your own unique sounds!

 

Price: US$95


Why we love it

  • A unique way to make music, and you could add a very interesting/interactive aspect to your performance
  • Very affordable price for the several versatile features
  • Combine with other MIDI controllers or instruments to create complex arrangements and performances. 
  • Very portable (can be connected to your computer, tablet, or smartphone via Bluetooth for a wireless setup)


Cons

  • Not best for very precise control


5. TouchMe


TouchMe allows you to connect to objects in the same way as Playtron. However, this is a sensory device as it is set to react differently to the touch. Meaning, when the area of touch and intensity changes, the sound changes too, causing the pitch to go higher or lower!

 

Price: US$99


Why we love it

  • Overall great sequencer
  • Perfect amount of keys
  • Combine with other MIDI controllers or instruments to create complex arrangements and performances. 
  • Explore the theremin-like sound generation potential of the TouchMe device to create dynamic and expressive music. 
  • Interact with other people in a musical creative environment.
  • Very portable (can be connected to your computer, tablet, or smartphone via Bluetooth for a wireless setup)


Cons

  • Not best for very precise control


6. Birth month stone guitar pick


Additionally, these hand-made picks are carved from smooth polished stone, guaranteed to tug on her heartstrings.

Stone options range from fiery red agate for July, soothing blue jasper for September, and bright yellow aventurine for November. Furthermore, each guitar pick will be totally unique thanks to the hand-carved natural stones used to make them. In addition, they are perfect for the minimalist mom that has it all!

 

Buy now


7. Concert tickets

This one might seem like a given. There’s little your mom will appreciate more than spending quality time with one of her favorite people (you!). Why not do that in a way that pays homage to her musical passion? 

Get Mom and yourself tickets to see her favorite band or artist. Mom will surely remember the evening for years to come (preferably something that ends before 8 pm!). Trust us on this. 


Make this Mother’s Day unforgettable

Regardless of your Mom's taste in music, all these Mother’s Day gifts are guaranteed to sound like music to her ears. Pair them with a hot breakfast in bed, and you'll be her favorite child for a long time!

items.title: Why ear training is important and how to get started
items.editorNotes: The article was rewritten for clarity and readability while maintaining the original content and links.
items.excerptBefore: In this article, we explain why ear training is important, and also show you methods to get started immediately.
items.excerptAfter: Discover the importance of ear training for musicians and learn how to get started with effective exercises.
items.seoTitleBefore: Why ear training is important and how to get started
items.seoTitleAfter: Why Ear Training is Important and How to Get Started
items.seoDescriptionBefore: In this article, we explain why ear training is important, and also show you methods to get started immediately.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore the significance of ear training for musicians and find out how to enhance your listening skills with practical exercises.
items.beforeBody:
By Just Rijna from StringKick

Learning to play music today is more convenient than ever. YouTube is filled with free tutorials and lessons that can help you learn almost any song you want. Compare that to what musicians did a few decades ago: they’d painstakingly work out how to play a song by carefully listening to a record or a tape. Clearly, we have it a lot better today!

 

But there is a downside to having YouTube and other resources just a few clicks away. Learning songs by ear is a great way to train your ears, which many musicians are now missing out on. And because it’s no longer necessary to learn songs by ear, many players skip over ear training altogether.

In this article, I’ll explain why that’s a mistake. We’ll explore two reasons why training your ears is important, and I’ll show you how you can best get started.

Reason 1: Ear training changes the way you listen to music

Why do you like the music you like? It’s a simple question, but not easy to answer. Most listeners without trained ears will focus on the parts that are easy to hear, such as vocals and guitar riffs. But by training your ears, you can learn to perceive music in much greater detail. The shifting harmonies… How the melody develops... How a bass line brings a track to life…

Learning to hear in more detail will deepen your appreciation of your favorite music, as you start to notice all the intricacies that make it so great. You might even start to appreciate artists or genres that didn’t appeal to you before!

This is also one of the many reasons why it is critical to protect your hearing. You want to be able to enjoy music down to the smallest detail today and for years to come. Look for hearing protection designed specifically for musicians that reduce volume without distorting the sound, such as Minuendo.

Buy now

Reason 2: Ear training is key to your development as a musician

Many musicians hit a certain plateau after a couple of years. I often hear from guitarists who can play many songs and have picked up some theory along the way. But somehow they don’t feel like they can actually ‘play’. Instead, they feel like they’re only memorizing which notes to play and never reached the point where they can let musical ideas flow.

What’s missing from these musicians is a solid connection between their hands and their musical minds. And that connection is developed through ear training. As you train your ears, more and more you will get a sense of freedom on your instrument to play what you hear in your head. This ability impacts pretty much every area of your musicianship: from improvisation to songwriting. It’s one of the most rewarding things you can learn as a musician.

How to start training your ears

So, we’ve discussed two important reasons to train your ears. But how do you get going?

Exercise 1:

Take your best pair of headphones and find a relaxing spot to sit. Now hit play on a song that you love and try to really listen to it. Let yourself be totally absorbed by the music. Focus and ask yourself what you’re hearing. How many instruments do you hear? Pick one of them. What is it playing? Try to follow it from the start of the track to the very end, note by note. Next, do the same for another instrument.

As you listen with total focus, you’re bound to discover things you never really noticed before. You can do this anytime you listen to music: give it your full attention and notice every little detail.

Exercise 2:

I recommend the approach that all the greatest musicians in music history took: learn songs by ear. I know this can seem a little intimidating at first. The trick is to start simple. Take a melody that you know well, that’s slow and doesn’t have too many notes.

Music teachers often suggest nursery rhymes for this. But children’s songs are often surprisingly complicated, not to mention not very inspiring to play. Instead, I’d recommend songs with simple riffs or melodies.

Examples include:

  • Breezeblocks by Alt-J
  • Satisfaction by the Rolling Stones
  • I Predict a Riot by Kaiser Chiefs

There’s a lot to be said about figuring out a song by ear, but the basics are simple. Listen closely to the first note. Sing or hum that note. Then find it on your instrument. This will take time the first time you do it. But you’ll be surprised at how much better you get at this within a few weeks of practice.

Final thoughts

Ear training can transform the way you listen to, appreciate, and create music. It can help you perceive music in greater detail. It can also develop your ability to play whatever you hear in your head – a skill that impacts almost every area of your playing. In short, ear training can open the door to a more meaningful and rewarding musical path. So give it a shot!

 

Just Rijna is the founder of StringKick, an online training academy for guitar players. StringKick is focused on helping you to train your ears, learn music theory and develop your musicality, all with the goal of making your musical journey more fun and fulfilling.
