items.title: Adrian Valia's music theory made simple: Soundbrenner review
items.editorNotes: Ensure all links are functional and relevant to the content.
items.excerptBefore: We review our popular affiliate and award winning multi-instrumentalist Adrian Valia’s new book "Music Theory Made Simple"
items.excerptAfter: Adrian Valia’s 'Music Theory Made Simple' offers a straightforward approach to music theory, making it accessible for all musicians. With interactive tools and a colorful layout, it demystifies concepts like rhythm…
items.seoTitleBefore: Adrian Valia's music theory made simple: Soundbrenner review
items.seoTitleAfter: Adrian Valia's Music Theory Made Simple: A Review
items.seoDescriptionBefore: We review our popular affiliate and award-winning multi-instrumentalist Adrian Valia’s new book "Music Theory Made Simple"
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Discover Adrian Valia's 'Music Theory Made Simple', an engaging book that simplifies music theory concepts. Perfect for musicians looking to enhance their…
items.beforeBody:
Finding Music Practice Intimidating?

Music theory can be intimidating, and oftentimes musicians aren’t too sure where to start. Adrian Valia’s ‘Music Theory Made Simple’ is an educational book that gives the reader a simple yet in-depth approach to music theory. With its colorful layout, interactive tools, QR codes and quizzes, it's a fun and intuitive way of learning music theory.

Adrian is an award-winning multi-instrumentalist jazz theorist, composer, and instructor. He’s also one of our favorite music content creators. Last summer, we collaborated with Adrian to discuss the importance of rhythm and how he uses metronome and Core in his workflow.

“Music Theory Made Simple” thoroughly demystifies music theory in a unique and approachable way. It’s perfect for anyone who has an interest in progressing their music theory knowledge and unlocking new creative potential. Its chapters comprise three main topics: rhythm, melody and harmony. With deep dives into rhythm basics and interval inversions, the circle of 5ths and modulations to name but a few — don't be scared of the fancy terminology, this book will take you through everything step by step!

Final Thoughts…

On a more personal note, Adrian Valia’s ‘Music Theory Made Simple’ book has a certain authenticity and personality that's sometimes rare to find in other educational books. Adrian stresses that music theory is a way to communicate our emotions and for him “it's a set of tools that allows you to understand and articulate the language of music,” and not just something for the elite. 

We highly recommend following his Instagram page for a better example of his colorful and intuitive teaching style (@adrian.valia). Or better still, just get the book and let your music journey begin! 

Adrian Valia’s ‘Music Theory Made Simple’ is available for US$21.99 OR US$11.99 for the Kindle version. You can find it globally on Amazon or the following bookshops for US and Canada.

US:

Barnes and NobleBook DepositoryBooks a MillionIndie Bound


CA:

Indigo

items.beforeBody:
Gifting is an art form in itself, especially when it comes to surprising someone that has a knack for singing! With all the options out there, it can be hard to pick a present that’s high quality and truly useful for singers. We’ve done the hard work for you by combing the internet for the top 5 best music-themed gifts to help you shortlist the perfect one for singers.

At Soundbrenner, we’re experts in everything music-related! Here’s our list of the 5 best gifts for singers, guaranteed to please singers of all ages and interests!

1. Flow microphone

This Flow microphone by Soundbrenner and that special singer friend of yours will go together like Bonnie and Clyde. This feature-packed mic records studio-quality sound up to 24-bit/96kHz and is compatible with iOS, Android, macOS & Windows systems. 

That's not all. It charges directly from the device it’s plugged into, so there is no need to wait around! Moreover, it minimizes background sound to give you that clean, professional sound!

Buy now 

2. Genki Wave

This wireless MIDI controller ring can be fully customized and is discrete enough to become an extension of their hand. It’s designed to let singers improve their practice sessions and help them make a real connection with their music and audience. 

What’s tiny yet packs a big punch and will surely make an excellent gift for anyone into singing? The Genki Wave gives singers Doctor Strange-level powers when it comes to controlling things with a flip of their hands. 

Buy now

3. LEVOIT Ultrasonic Humidifier

Gotta keep those vocal cords healthy! Show your singer how much you appreciate them by pampering them with this humidifier by LEVOIT.

It can be filled up with 4 liters of water, is whisper quiet, and doubles up as a diffuser for an extra soothing touch. It has a built-in safety shut-off feature for when the water reservoir empties. Priced at under $50, it's a must-have for all singers and easy on the pocket too!

Buy now

4. Song-themed board game

Still, looking for the best gift for singers? Here's an idea guaranteed to be a hit with more than one of those upbeat, playful singers in your life. A board game that lets them practice their songs and spend some quality time with loved ones! Spontaneous is a family favorite, especially if there’s more than one singer in yours!

Buy now

5. Charm bracelet 

On a budget? With this high-quality, customizable charm bracelet for under $19, no one will be the wiser. You can add their birthstone along with the elegant silver microphone charm for a unique gift that will remind them of you. 

The bracelet is resizable, so it can be gifted to music lovers of any age. Plus, it's shipped in a velvet jewelry pouch perfect for gifting, so there is no need to scramble around for gift wrap.

Buy now

items.title: A year end letter from Soundbrenner
items.editorNotes: Revised for clarity and readability while maintaining factual content.
items.excerptBefore: 2021 has been an exciting year for Soundbrenner. Check out the highlights from Florian, CEO and Founder of Soundbrenner.
items.excerptAfter: A year-end letter from Soundbrenner reflecting on a busy 2021, including product launches and updates.
items.seoTitleBefore: A year end letter from Soundbrenner
items.seoTitleAfter: A Year-End Letter from Soundbrenner
items.seoDescriptionBefore: 2021 has been an exciting year for Soundbrenner. Check out the highlights from Florian, CEO and Founder of Soundbrenner.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Read about Soundbrenner's achievements in 2021, including the launch of Soundbrenner Plus, Minuendo earplugs, and more.
items.beforeBody:
2021 is almost over and wow, has it been a busy year!

We kicked off the year with the launch of Soundbrenner Plus for iOS. Consequently, it brought many of the most often requested features to our iOS app. Additionally, everyone can now easily generate a link to share a song or a full set list. Furthermore, you don't even have to be a subscriber for sharing or importing songs and set lists this way! On top of that, the full library is now synchronized across all your devices where you're logged in with your Soundbrenner account.

Soundbrenner Plus

We're excited to announce that our engineers have been hard at work to bring Soundbrenner Plus to Android, and it will be ready in Q1 next year! Best of all, it will come with all the same features as for iOS and a full redesign of the Library that adds many features that had previously been missing on Android. Plus, the whole library will be cross-platform supported, so you can share songs between iOS & Android devices and even log in both types of devices and keep the library completely in sync.

Minuendo Lossless Earplugs

After Soundbrenner Plus, we launched earplugs from our partner brand Minuendo, which, remarkably, have received a smashing reception. Wow! We could never have expected this!

Many musicians love the variable slider to adjust the volume without taking the earplugs out. Additionally, they appreciate the many tip options and more. If you don't know what I'm talking about, definitely check out the Minuendo.

Stomp Bluetooth foot pedal

After the success of Minuendo we partnered with Coda Music to launch their Stomp Bluetooth foot pedal on our store. With that came Android support for Bluetooth foot pedals to our app (which was already supported prior to our iOS app). We even launched a special Coda x Soundbrenner special edition of Stomp in teal color.

It's been great to introduce our favorite music tech products to the Soundbrenner community - expect more to come in the near future!

Core

We also launched several free software updates for Core. For example, we added a highly requested Alarm feature to the Core via a firmware update. You can now create silent, vibrating alarms on the Core. Moreover, you can even automatically repeat them on certain weekdays. All of this can be done from the convenience of your wrist.
Thanks to the community for these awesome suggestions. You can make your own suggestions and upvote what we should build next on.

We are already hard at work on the next features and more free updates will be released in 2022!

Towards the end of the year, we released our first special edition for the Core Steel that is limited to just 777 units. Additionally, comes with a special engraving, a handwritten card with your unit number and most importantly a special sandblasted silver finish paired with an Italian, brown leather strap. We're almost sold out, so make sure to grab one if this sounds like something you would love! https://www.soundbrenner.com/shop-core 

2021 was clearly Soundbrenner's best year yet, but 2022 is bound to be even more exciting. Stay tuned for many more product launches and software updates to come. In the meantime, what would you like to see next? Additionally, how was 2021 for you? I would love to hear from you. Feel free to reach me via florian@soundbrenner.com.

Sincerely,
Florian
Founder & CEO of Soundbrenner

Plus, the entire library will be cross-platform supported, allowing you to share songs between iOS and Android devices while keeping the library completely in sync.

Minuendo Lossless Earplugs

After Soundbrenner Plus, we launched earplugs from our partner brand Minuendo, which have received an incredible reception. Many musicians love the variable slider to adjust the volume without removing the earplugs. Additionally, they appreciate the various tip options and more. If you haven't checked out Minuendo yet, we highly recommend it.

Stomp Bluetooth foot pedal

Following the success of Minuendo, we partnered with Coda Music to launch their Stomp Bluetooth foot pedal in our store. This also introduced Android support for Bluetooth foot pedals to our app, which was already available for iOS. We even launched a special Coda x Soundbrenner edition of Stomp in teal color. It's been fantastic to introduce our favorite music tech products to the Soundbrenner community, and we expect more to come in the near future!

Core

We also launched several free software updates for Core. For example, we added a highly requested Alarm feature via a firmware update. You can now create silent, vibrating alarms on the Core, which can automatically repeat on certain weekdays. All of this can be done conveniently from your wrist. Thanks to the community for these awesome suggestions! You can make your own suggestions and upvote what we should build next.

Towards the end of the year, we released our first special edition for the Core Steel, limited to just 777 units. This edition comes with a special engraving, a handwritten card with your unit number, and a unique sandblasted silver finish paired with an Italian brown leather strap. We're almost sold out, so make sure to grab one if this sounds like something you would love! Shop Core

2021 was clearly Soundbrenner's best year yet, but 2022 is bound to be even more exciting. Stay tuned for many more product launches and software updates to come. In the meantime, what would you like to see next? Additionally, how was 2021 for you? I would love to hear from you. Feel free to reach me via florian@soundbrenner.com.

Sincerely,

Florian

Founder & CEO of Soundbrenner

items.beforeBody:
Flutes generally get a rep for being low-maintenance instruments, but that particular flute player in your life can do with some high-quality presents every now and then! Here is our selection of the 10 best gifts for flute players!

Best gifts for flute players: Our top 10 picks

We’ve made picking the perfect gift easier for any flute enthusiast you might want to surprise! Here are the best gifts for flute players ideas that will set them up for playing some flutetastic music:

1. Soundbrenner Pulse

Buy now

 

Get your favourite flute player on their way to becoming the next Ian Anderson by surprising them with the Soundbrenner Pulse. This vibrating wearable metronome makes the ideal present for any musician looking to improve their craft.

2. SKYNEO Flute Cleaning Kit

Buy now

Everyone likes being pampered, even flutes! This pocket-friendly, multi-functional flute cleaning and repair kit is just for that special someone and their favorite instrument!

3. Chapstick

Buy now

 

What do all flute players have in common? Love for their craft and chapped lips! But practicing the flute with dry lips is no joke. So gift them a set of these musician-approved Gosling's Original chapsticks to keep them on their game.

4. Sky Flute case

Buy now

 

Help your flute player carry their precious cargo in style with this hard flute case. It's padded, waterproof, and ultra-lightweight. Plus, this small business offers multiple colors and styles to suit any personality.

5. Flute lessons

Buy now

 

Still not sure what to buy? Lessons by ArtistWorks will surely be a solid hit with any flute player! The platform offers hundreds of pre-recorded flute lessons by expert musicians to help flute players at any experience level improve their craft.

6. Charm bracelet 

Buy now

 

Looking to score some brownie points with the perfect flute-themed present? Look no further. This high-quality Rolo chain bracelet delivers on the timeless fashion front while being easy on your pocket. Win-win!

7. Flow microphone

Buy now

 

Is that flute player of yours still fiddling around with bulky microphones? (Pun intended!) Gift them the Flow microphone so they can deliver their best performance yet!

8. Sheet music stand

Buy now

 

Remember that infamous scene from the TV show “Friends” in which Ross tries to impress his girlfriend by playing the flute and puts her to sleep instead? Surprise your loved one with this folding sheet music stand to help them avoid a similar fate. 

With this lightweight stand, they’ll always have the right sheet music handy for any occasion! It's compact, easy to fold and carry, and designed for professionals.

9. Minuendo Earplugs

Buy now

Are you looking for the best gift for flute players that’s also minimalistic and stylish? These Minuendo Earplugs offer superior adjustable hearing protection, making practice sessions more manageable and safer. Their discreet design makes them the perfect gift for the flute player that has it all!

10. Classical Sheet music book

Buy now

 

If they love their flute, they will surely love this timeless classic as well. This collection has 30+ classical flute solos featuring some of the best composers including Beethoven, Bach, Satie, Chopin, Mozart, and Vivaldi. 

This book offers the right blend of compositions for flute players at beginner, intermediate, and advanced levels.

items.beforeBody:
With so many choices, buying the best electric guitar under $300, under $500 and under $2000 can be impossible. We've done the work for you!

Looking to understand guitar specs first before buying? We’ve got you covered here in this article.

Best electric guitar under $300

Squier Affinity Stratocaster

  • Body: Alder
  • Neck: Maple, C-shaped
  • Fretboard: Maple
  • Frets: 21
  • Pickups: Squier Single-Coil Strat

Options for sub-$300 guitars have traditionally been limited (and not great), but luckily modern guitarists are presented with a wide range of options.

The guitar of choice for many beginning guitarists, the Squier series is an affordable gateway into the world of Fender guitars.

The Affinity series offers a classic Stratocaster body, along with single-coil pickups and a C-shaped maple neck, making it great for achieving vintage tones on a tight budget.

Ibanez RG421

  • Body: Meranti
  • Neck: Maple Wizard III
  • Fretboard: Jatoba
  • Frets: 24
  • Pickups: Quantum humbuckers

The Ibanez RG421 is a great guitar for beginners or those on a budget. It features a thin and fast maple Wizard neck as well as Quantum humbucking pickups. Unlike some of Ibanez’s other models, the RG421 has a fixed bridge, making it great for beginners. This is an excellent choice for those looking for speed and playability in the best electric guitar.

Yamaha Pacifica 112v

  • Body: Alder
  • Neck: Maple, C-shaped
  • Fretboard: Rosewood
  • Frets: 22
  • Pickups: Yamaha Alnico V Single-Coil

The Pacifica 112v is an extremely affordable electric guitar from Yamaha with a C-shaped neck, rosewood fretboard and 22 frets. Another great feature at this price point is the H/S/S configuration, which gives you the versatility to play a wide range of styles.

Best electric guitar from between $500-$1,000

This price range is normally where you get the best value for money. Guitars in this category come packed with features at a price that is still highly affordable. Because you are paying slightly more, there won’t be as many corners cut when it comes to building quality or specifications.

Fender Player Series Stratocaster

  • Body: Alder
  • Neck: Maple, Modern C
  • Fretboard: Pau Ferro
  • Frets: 22
  • Pickups: Player Series Alnico 5 Strat Single-Coil

The Player series by Fender represents the best value in their entire lineup at the moment, providing excellent specs and modern features at a great price. This Mexican-made Strat comes with an alder body, a Modern-C neck profile and three single coil pickups, as well as 22 frets on a maple fretboard. This is essentially the best Stratocaster you can get for under $1,000, with specs good enough for beginners and professionals alike.

ESP LTD EC-1000 

  • Body: Mahogany
  • Neck: Mahogany Thin U
  • Fretboard: Pau Ferro
  • Frets: 24
  • Pickups: EMG 80/61

Despite looking like a classic Gibson Les Paul, this ESP guitar has been built from the ground up for high-intensity shredding. 

Besides offering 24 frets instead of 22, the EC-1000 also comes fitted with a set of EMG 60/81 active pickups, allowing for extremely thick high-gain tones. When paired with the right amp or distortion pedal, these pickups will truly pack a punch. The ebony fretboard and comfortable mahogany neck are also ideal for speedy guitar solos. If you’re looking for the perfect metal guitar under $1,000, this is it.

PRS SE Custom 24

  • Body: Mahogany with Beveled Maple Top and Flame Maple Veneer
  • Neck: Maple, Wide Thin
  • Fretboard: Rosewood
  • Frets: 24
  • Pickups: 85/15 “S”

While PRS are best known for their high-end US-made guitars, they also offer an SE line with affordable Korean-made guitars. The SE Custom 24 is one of these. With a beautiful mahogany body and maple top, as well as 85/15 pickups that offer both humbucking and single-coil tones, this is a highly versatile all-rounder. Playability is also excellent, thanks to the Wide, Thin maple neck and rosewood fretboard. If you’re looking for a guitar that can do it all for under $1,000, you can’t go wrong with this one.

Best electric guitar between $1,000-$2000 

PRS CE24

  • Body: Mahogany with maple top
  • Neck: Maple, Pattern Thin
  • Fretboard: Rosewood
  • Frets: 24
  • Pickups: 85/15 

Although it comes in at just under $2,000, the CE24 is still one of PRS’s more affordable models. At this price point, you get a US-built guitar for half the price of comparable models from PRS. Key specifications include a bolt-on maple neck, 85/15 pickups, and of course 24 frets. Compared to the even more affordable SE Custom 24, the CE24 features a thinner neck, higher-quality electronics, and a flamed maple top compared to the SE’s veneer. The neck on the CE24 has a smooth satin finish, while the SE’s is glossy. Another important difference is that the CE24 features a bolt-on neck, while the SE has a set neck. Overall, the CE24 stands out as one of the best electric guitars you can get at this price point.

Fender American Professional II Telecaster

  • Body: Alder
  • Neck: Maple, Deep C
  • Fretboard: Rosewood
  • Frets: 22
  • Pickups: V-Mod II Single-Coil Tele

The new Professional II Telecaster features a bolt-on Deep-C neck, 22 frets, and V Mod II Tele pickups. In terms of woods, the body is made from alder, with a maple neck and rosewood fretboard. It also comes with a sculpted neck that makes it easier to reach higher frets. For the money, this is one of the best electric guitars you can buy.

items.title: 6 Alternative Ways for Musicians to Make Money
items.editorNotes: The article has been rewritten for clarity and readability while maintaining the original factual content and required links.
items.excerptBefore: Are you a musician looking for a new way to make money? Here we are recommending 6 alternative ways for you to make money.
items.excerptAfter: Explore six alternative ways for musicians to generate income, including livestream performances, selling merchandise, and starting a blog.
items.seoTitleBefore: 6 Alternative Ways for Musicians to Make Money
items.seoTitleAfter: 6 Alternative Ways for Musicians to Make Money
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Are you a musician looking for a new way to make money? Here we are recommending 6 alternative ways for you to make money.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Discover six effective methods for musicians to earn income, from livestream performances to creating a YouTube channel and teaching music classes.
items.beforeBody:
Written by Micah Norris

The entertainment industry has been ruined by the Coronavirus Pandemic. According to the BBC, in 2020, music creators lost two-thirds of their income, and the shutdown of concerts and festivals caused live music revenues to fall by 85%. It may be years before live music returns to normalcy, so musicians are increasingly looking for alternative ways to make money. Our team at Soundbrenner recommends six ways in which musicians can make money.

Livestream performances

In the absence of live shows, online performances fill a gap for those who crave access to live music. If you are comfortable performing to a virtual audience, then charging a viewing or admission fee for online shows is one of the alternative ways to make money and earn some extra income on the side.

Sell merchandise

If you are a well-known musician in your local area, consider selling merchandise. Your star has yet to reach zenith - but fun and quirky merchandise will always be popular. Use your spare time to create a logo or interesting artworks to sell as posters, shirts, hats, or other memorabilia as alternative ways to make money.

Create a YouTube channel

A YouTube channel is an excellent vehicle for getting your music out into the public eye, but it is not the only benefit. By joining the YouTube Partner Programme, you can use ads on your videos to generate income.

Teach a class

If you have excellent music skills, set yourself up as a music teacher. You can do it face to face, under COVID-friendly conditions, or virtually. You can conduct lessons via Zoom or sell pre-recorded lessons through an educational site such as Udemy. Teach music theory or production, depending on where your talents lie. 

Start a blog

Blogging can take a few months to generate an income. Starting a blog is one of the top alternative ways to make money. Top blogger Pat Flynn of Smart Passive Income earns around $2 million a year. Starting a blog is as simple as signing up to a hosting provider and writing engaging articles. Affiliate links and advertising can provide significant passive income if you drive enough traffic to your website. 

Consider starting an educational blog where you teach an instrument. A local scene blog that lets readers know about gigs and concerts in the local area, or a gossip blog that dishes celebrity dirt. Your blog can be a great place to implement some of the above ideas and explore alternative ways to make money - such as streaming your show or selling merchandise.

Freelancing opportunities

Your heart may belong to the music, but have you thought about nonmusical freelancing opportunities as alternative ways to make money? Consider being a Virtual Assistant, trying freelance writing, setting up as an accountant, or putting those IT skills to use. It’s easier to find remote work so that you can use other skills without having to use an office.

These are tough days for musicians and the live music industry. Creating and formalizing alternative ways to make money can give you the freedom to retain your freedom and integrity whilst earning a decent living wage.

Are you looking for more guides and tips to take your music to the next level?

Check out more articles more here.

Contributor

Micah Norris created king-rom.com after having trouble finding quality ROMs. He decided to build a site where he could compile the best sources of ROMs online. As a gamer he understands that PS1, PS2, Nintendo 64, Gameboy C/A, Nintendo DS, NES, and SNES ROMs are hard to find from trusted sources. He encourages gamers, new and old, to check out the ones he has found. Besides information on ROMs, he also writes about gaming and resources.
